15 April 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Pakistani Army Chief Arrives In Tehran To 'Plan Second Round of Iran-US Talks' RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- Iranian state media reported that Pakistani Army Commander Asim Munir had arrived in Tehran on April 16 as the head of a high-ranking political-security delegation.
  • April 15: Iran-US ceasefire set to extend to Lebanon as Tehran shapes regional diplomacy Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Regional diplomacy gathered momentum on Wednesday as Iran's growing influence steered toward a ceasefire in Lebanon, reportedly set to take effect soon.
  • April 14: Fragile ceasefire holds as US threat of 'naval blockade' in Strait of Hormuz fails Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- On April 14, despite US threats of a "naval blockade," Iranian-linked vessels continued to transit the Strait of Hormuz, with maritime trackers confirming multiple crossings, including a US-sanctioned Chinese tanker.

  • Report: Pentagon Sends Thousands More Troops To Middle East RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- The Pentagon is deploying thousands of additional troops to the Middle East as the Trump administration seeks to pressure Iran into a deal while preparing for possible escalation if a fragile cease-fire collapses, The Washington Post reports.
  • U.S. military claims economic trade of Iran by sea "completely halted" People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. military said on Tuesday that U.S. forces have completely halted economic trade going into and out of Iran by sea in less than 36 hours since the blockade was implemented.
  • US military: 400 troops wounded in Iranian reprisal strikes Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- The US military has updated the number of casualties from its joint aggression with Israel against Iran.
  • US Navy Confirms 'mishap' to $250 million spy drone downed by Iran Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- The US Navy has confirmed that an MQ-4C Triton unmanned surveillance aircraft crashed in the Persian Gulf region on April 9, with the incident now described as a mishap, although little was revealed regarding the circumstance under which it was lost.
  • Over 10,000 U.S. troops enforce blockade on vessels to and from Iranian ports People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Central Command said Tuesday that more than 10,000 troops are executing the mission to blockade ships entering and departing Iranian ports, impartially targeting vessels of all nations.
  • US CENTCOM Says Blockade Against Iranian Maritime Trade 'Fully Implemented' RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- US Central Command (CENTCOM) says a blockade of Iranian ports has been "fully implemented," cutting off vital trade for the country's economy.
  • US Military Says 'No Ships Made It Past' Naval Blockade Targeting Iran RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- The US military said that no ships slipped through a naval blockade targeting vessels headed to or from Iran in the first 24 hours of the restrictive measure, while tracking-service data indicated that a few Iran-linked ships exited the Strait of Hormuz during that time period.

  • Iran Acquired Chinese Spy Satellite To Monitor US Bases, Says FT Report RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- Iran secretly acquired a Chinese-built spy satellite, giving it new capabilities to monitor and potentially target US military bases across the Middle East, the Financial Times reports.

  • Trump Says War Could End 'Very Soon,' Top Pakistani Mediator Travels To Iran RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said the war with Iran could be over "very soon," and Pakistan's powerful army chief visited Tehran in a bid to bridge the gap between the United States and Iran and pave the way for a new round of talks.
  • Bessent Confirms US Won't Renew Iran, Russia Oil Waivers RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- Washington will not be renewing waivers that had enabled purchases of Iranian and Russian oil exempt from US sanctions, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on April 15.
  • U.S. Upends Iranian Shadow Fleet and Oil-for-Gold Terror Financing Network US Dept. of State 15 Apr 2026 -- The United States is acting to decisively limit Iran's ability to generate revenue as it attempts to hold the Strait of Hormuz hostage.
  • Economic Fury Targets Illicit Oil Smuggling Network Run by Iranian Regime Elite US Treasury 15 Apr 2026 -- Today, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) intensified pressure on Iran's illicit oil transportation infrastructure by sanctioning more than two dozen individuals, companies, and vessels operating within the network of Iranian oil shipping magnate Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani (Shamkhani), the son of now-deceased senior Iranian security official Ali Shamkhani.
  • Trump Says Peace Talks Could Resume Soon As US Military Chokes Iran's Maritime Trade RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump suggested that peace talks with Iran could resume in a day or two as the US military chokes off the country's maritime trade in a move to get Tehran to the negotiating table.
  • US lawmakers move to impeach War Secretary Hegseth over Iran 'war crimes' Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- A number of US lawmakers are set to file five articles of impeachment against Secretary of War Pete Hegseth over war crimes committed in Iran.
  • Trump says not thinking about extending ceasefire with Iran People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that he wasn't thinking about extending the ceasefire with Iran.
  • Trump claims Iran war "very close" to completion People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump claimed on Tuesday that the war against Iran is "very close" to completion.

  • PM Netanyahu on the Situations with Lebanon and Iran Israel - Prime Minister 15 Apr 2026

  • Profiles: Top commanders and officials martyred in 40-day US-Israeli war on Iran Press TV 15 Apr 2026
  • Araghchi thanks Pakistan for hosting Iran-US talks as he receives army chief Asim Munir Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said in a social media post that he thanked Pakistan for its "gracious hosting" of the US-Iran dialogue as he received the country's Army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir in Tehran on Wednesday.
  • 'Iran not blockadable': Tehran says all 'means of pressure' will fail Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Iran is "not blockadable," the Foreign Ministry spokesman says, rejecting the US attempt to impose a naval blockade on the country, while warning that it violates the fragile ceasefire and could trigger a proportional response from Iranian armed forces.
  • Iran's top general warns US over illegal naval blockade in Persian Gulf Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Iran's top military commander, Major General Ali Abdollahi, has issued a stern warning to the United States, stating that any attempt to block maritime trade in the Persian Gulf or Sea of Oman would be met with decisive action.
  • No power can force Iranian nation into submission: Pezeshkian Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian says no power in the world can force the Iranian nation into submission, slamming double standards in international policies.
  • Iran rejects UAE proposal on 'maritime corridor' in Strait of Hormuz Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Iran has vehemently rejected a UAE-backed proposal adopted by the International Maritime Organization (IMO) on establishing a so-called "safe maritime corridor" in the Strait of Hormuz, slamming it as legally-groundless and politically-motivated.
  • Iran condemns US 'misrepresentation' of UN chief report at UNSC Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Iran's ambassador and permanent representative to the United Nations, Amir Saeed Iravani, has sent a letter to the world body and the UN Security Council (UNSC) condemning US ambassador's "misleading" representation of Secretary General's report regarding the situation in West Asia.

  • Middle East conflict chokes end of supply chain as lights go out in the Pacific UN News 15 Apr 2026 -- For Pacific Island countries, the Middle East crisis is not a distant geopolitical event. It is already showing up in higher fuel prices, electricity uncertainty and fears that communities sitting at the far end of global supply chains could be pushed into deeper economic insecurity.
  • Resumption of U.S.-Iran talks probable: UN chief People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres said Tuesday that the resumption of talks between Washington and Tehran is highly probable.

  • China urges resumption of Hormuz traffic, warns of global economic, humanitarian risks from conflict Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 Apr 2026 -- China on Wednesday called for the immediate resumption of maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and warned of broader economic and humanitarian risks from the conflict involving the United States and Iran, Anadolu Agency reported.

  • Russia backs Iran's 'inalienable right' to uranium enrichment: Lavrov Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov has defended Iran's inalienable right to enrich uranium for peaceful purposes, saying that Russia accepts any decision that suits the Iranian side.
  • Russia's Uranium Removal Proposal For Iran Was Good Solution, But US Rejected It - Kremlin Sputnik 15 Apr 2026 -- The initiative to transport enriched uranium from Iran to Russia is not currently under discussion, but Russian President Vladimir Putin is ready to return to it, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Wednesday.

  • Press remarks by President António Costa following the visit to United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia and Qatar EC/CEU 15 Apr 2026
  • Japan-Iran Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 15 Apr 2026
  • Turkey's Erdogan: Israel must not be allowed to sabotage US-Iran ceasefire Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has warned against the Israeli regime's attempts to sabotage a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, saying that Ankara will push for an extension of the truce and ensure peace talks continue in Pakistan.
  • HRH the Crown Prince, European Council President Discuss Saudi-EU Strategic Partnership, Regional Developments SPA 15 Apr 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, met in Jeddah yesterday with President of the European Council António Costa
  • PH, Oman hope for de-escalation of Middle East conflict Philippine News Agency 15 Apr 2026 -- The Philippines and Oman have expressed hope for the peaceful and diplomatic resolution of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

  • Azeem Ibrahim: Iran Conflict Will Be Decided By Who Can 'Endure The Most' RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- Developments around the Strait of Hormuz are moving fast, two days after the start of a US naval blockade.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Counter-UAS Marketplace Streamlines Acquisition, First Purchases Total $13 Million DoD News 15 Apr 2026 -- Joint Interagency Task Force 401 announced today the first set of purchases completed through its newly launched counter-unmanned aircraft systems marketplace. The four purchases, collectively valued at $13 million, represent a new milestone in the task force's mission to streamline critical counter-UAS technology acquisition among the War Department and its interagency partners.
  • U.S. Army announces popular name for the MV-75 FLRAA: "Cheyenne II" US Army 15 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Army announced today that its next-generation multi-role vertical lift aircraft, the MV-75 Future Long Range Assault Aircraft, will carry the Native American name "Cheyenne II".
  • Marines conduct cargo UAS shipboard testing US Navy 15 Apr 2026 -- The Marine Corps recently conducted shipboard testing with the TRV-150C Tactical Resupply Unmanned Aircraft System (TRUAS) aboard a San Antonio-class amphibious transport dock (LPD), marking a significant milestone in advancing ship-to-shore and ship-to-ship resupply solutions for Combat Logistics Battalions (CLBs).
  • Remarks by Chief of Space Operations Gen. Chance Saltzman at Space Symposium United States Space Force 15 Apr 2026
  • CSO debuts Future Operating Environment, Objective Force at 41st Space Symposium United States Space Force 15 Apr 2026 -- Chief of Space Operations Gen. Chance Saltzman detailed a comprehensive vision for the service's next 15 years, publicly introducing the "Future Operating Environment" and the "Objective Force" that will prepare the service to win in an increasingly contested and complex domain.
  • Roaring into action: African Lion 2026 begins in Tunisia Army News 15 Apr 2026 -- Military forces from Tunisia, the United States and international partner nations officially inaugurated Exercise African Lion 2026, April 13. The multinational engagement in Tunisia, scheduled to conclude April 30, serves as a venue for strengthening regional security cooperation and refining the readiness of participating forces in a complex, global security environment.

  • NATO Strengthens Allied Search and Rescue Cooperation in the North Atlantic NATO Allied Maritime Command 15 Apr 2026 -- Dynamic Mercy 2026, an annual NATO led Search and Rescue (SAR) exercise, began April 15, bringing together Allied naval, air, and national maritime agencies to enhance coordination and interoperability for rescue operations in the challenging environment of the Northern Atlantic.
  • NATO envoys visit Seoul to discuss defense industry cooperation KOREA.net 15 Apr 2026 -- A visiting delegation of 30 NATO ambassadors has discussed bilateral cooperation with Korea in the defense industry at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs compound in Seoul.

  • Statement by Russian Defence Ministry Russia MoD 15 Apr 2026 -- Reportedly, on 26 March 2026, the leadership of a number of European countries took a decision to increase production and supply of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to Ukraine for launching strikes on Russian territory in the face of growing losses and an acute shortage of manpower in the Armed Forces of Ukraine.
  • Russian naval flotilla visits South China's Zhanjiang, showcasing routine exchanges and strategic trust: Chinese experts Global Times 15 Apr 2026 -- A Russian naval flotilla composed of the corvettes Sovershennyy and Rezkiy, along with the medium seagoing tanker Pechenga, arrived in Zhanjiang, South China's Guangdong Province, on Wednesday morning, kicking off a five-day friendly visit, according to Xinhua News Agency.
  • Chief of General Staff of Armed Forces of Russian Federation Valery Gerasimov holds talks with his Mongolian counterpart Russia MoD 15 Apr 2026 -- In Moscow, in the context of developing a bilateral military cooperation, Chief of General Staff of the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation / First Deputy Minister of Defence of the Russian Federation General of the Army Valery Gerasimov held talks with Chief of the General Staff of the Armed Forces of Mongolia Lieutenant General Sunrev Ganbyamba.

  • The President of Ukraine and the Minister of Defense of Italy Discussed Cooperation in the Defense Sector and Possible Formats of Such Partnership President of Ukraine 15 Apr 2026
  • UK announces biggest ever drone package for Ukraine to push back Putin UK MOD 15 Apr 2026 -- Biggest ever UK drone package for Ukraine announced, including more than 120,000 drones, supplying cutting-edge battlefield technology.
  • Air defence, drones, data exchange and joint production: Ukraine and Germany scale up cooperation Ukraine MOD 15 Apr 2026 -- Ukraine and Germany are scaling up key areas of defence cooperation, particularly in air defence, the exchange of battlefield data, the development of drone capabilities, and joint weapons production.
  • NATO Secretary General welcomes additional aid at Ukraine Defence Contact Group meeting NATO 15 Apr 2026 -- On Wednesday (15 April 2026) NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte participated in a meeting of the Ukraine Defence Contact Group (UDCG) in Berlin. The meeting was jointly chaired by German Minister of Defence, Boris Pistorius and UK Defence Secretary, John Healey.
  • Strengthening Ukraine's Air Defense and Agreements on Drone Deals: Results of the President's Visits to Germany and Norway President of Ukraine 15 Apr 2026
  • Ukraine proposes that Germany test new defence technologies under the Test in Ukraine platform Ukraine MOD 15 Apr 2026 -- Ukraine stands ready to share its unique combat experience and digital solutions with Germany, as well as to facilitate the testing of new defence technologies under the Test in Ukraine platform. Ukraine's Minister of Defence, Mykhailo Fedorov, discussed mechanisms for such cooperation during a meeting with members of the Defence Committee of the German Bundestag, led by Thomas Röwekamp.
  • Malloy T-150 octocopter: UK heavy-lift drone that helped destroy a bridge Ukraine MOD 15 Apr 2026 -- Malloy T-150 drones provided by the UK to Ukraine back in 2022 have proven reliable as 'workhorse' platforms, capable of performing logistics tasks even in challenging weather conditions.

  • China's homegrown 'unmanned flying truck' HH-200 completes maiden flight on Wednesday Global Times 15 Apr 2026 -- China achieved a new milestone in making large-scale unmanned transport equipment as the domestically-developed HH-200 successfully completed its maiden flight on Wednesday.

  • Interoperability crucial for Taiwan evacuation: Ex-Philippine Army official CNA 15 Apr 2026 -- Former Philippine Army vice commander Leodevic B. Guinid, in a recent interview, called for regional interoperability mechanisms for the evacuation of international noncombatants in the event of a Chinese attack against Taiwan.

  • Defence prepares for new National Defence Strategy Australia DOD 15 Apr 2026 -- A bigger, stronger and more lethal Defence Force is better placed to defend Australia and its interests, according to Chief of the Defence Force Admiral David Johnston.
  • Reciprocal naval visits deepen ties Australia DOD 15 Apr 2026 -- Reciprocal port visits between Australia and Vietnam have been conducted for the first time.

  • IOS SAGAR SHIP ARRIVES AT PHUKET, THAILAND India PIB 15 Apr 2026 -- Indian Ocean Ship (IOS) SAGAR arrived at Phuket, Thailand, on 14 Apr 2026 on completion of a six-day transit from Malé, Maldives, marking the second port call of its ongoing mission.
  • FIRST EDITION OF NAVAL COMMANDERS' CONFERENCE-2026 COMMENCES AT NAUSENA BHAWAN, NEW DELHI India PIB 15 Apr 2026 -- The Indian Navy Commanders' Conference 01/2026 commenced at Nausena Bhawan on 14 Apr 2026 with an inaugural address by Adm Dinesh K Tripathi, Chief of the Naval Staff, to senior naval leadership, outstation Operational and Area Commanders, and Command Headquarters and Naval Headquarters staff.

  • Belgium seizes two British cargoes of military components bound for Israel Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Belgian authorities have seized two British shipments of military components bound for Israel, invoking a ban on any military-laden aircraft using the country's facilities or airspace.

  • Japan-New Zealand Defense Ministerial Video Conference (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 15 Apr 2026
  • Japan- Türkiye Military to Military (MM) Dialogue Ministry of Defense of Japan 15 Apr 2026

  • UK military chiefs asked to find £3.5bn in savings - and get ready for war Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 Apr 2026 -- UK military chiefs have been asked to find £3.5bn in "efficiencies" and other savings this year, even as Sir Keir Starmer says he is readying his armed forces for war, according to Sky News.
  • 1,200 UK jobs supported by nearly £900 million defence deal to keep military helicopters mission-ready UK MOD 15 Apr 2026 -- More than 1,000 jobs have been secured through a new contract to help sustain Army Apache and RAF Chinook helicopters.

  • Moldova to exit post-Soviet CIS bloc in 2027, foreign minister says Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 Apr 2026 -- Moldovan Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mihai Popsoi said his country will officially cease to be a part of the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS), a post-Soviet regional bloc formed after the collapse of the Soviet Union, as of April 8, 2027, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • Further phase tasks successfully completed in "Anatolian Phoenix - 2026" exercise Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 Apr 2026 -- The tasks of the further phase of the "Anatolian Phoenix - 2026" International Search and Rescue Exercise, held in the city of Konya in brotherly Türkiye, have been successfully completed, Azerbaijan's Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • Commission to invest €1.07 billion in 57 defence projects supporting European Readiness Flagships European Commission 15 Apr 2026
  • EDA delivers value for Member States - Annual Report 2025 European Defence Agency 15 Apr 2026 -- The European Union's security environment has been demanding in recent years and 2025 was no exception. Yet 2025 was also a year in which the European Union demonstrated determination: to strengthen capabilities, sustain readiness, and respond effectively within our neighbourhood.
  • PESCO HIGHLIGHT: Integrated Unmanned Ground Systems (iUGS) Permanent Structured Cooperation (PESCO) 15 Apr 2026

  • Decree on Certain Documents Related to Export Control of Dual-Use Items enters into force Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Finland 15 Apr 2026 -- The Decree of the Ministry for Foreign Affairs on Certain Documents Related to Export Control of Dual-Use Items entered into force on 15 April 2026. The decree supplements the Act on the Export Control of Dual-Use Items (500/2024), which entered into force in 2024, and clarifies the reporting and notification obligations for exporters. The provisions of the decree concern reporting on the use of the global export authorisations and the content of the notifications and reports related to the internal compliance programme and the use of the Union general export authorisations.

  • The Presidents of Lithuania and Estonia: Security of the Baltic region Is an integral part of Europe's overall security President of the Republic of Lithuania 15 Apr 2026 -- On Tuesday, President Gitanas Nausėda met with the President of Estonia, Alar Karis, who is on a state visit to Lithuania. The Presidents discussed bilateral relations, strengthening regional security and defense, support for Ukraine, strategic energy and infrastructure projects, and the expansion of economic and cultural cooperation. This year marks the 105th anniversary of the establishment of diplomatic relations between Lithuania and Estonia and the 35th anniversary of their restoration.

  • A look at the Defence news 6 - 12 April Netherlands Ministry of Defence 15 Apr 2026

  • 'Balikatan' 2026 to include drone warfare defense - US spox Philippine News Agency 15 Apr 2026 -- Defending against hostile drone systems is among the exercises to be undertaken in this year's "Balikatan" drills.

  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Kyrgyz Republic Chairman of the Cabinet Adylbek Kasymaliev US Dept. of State 15 Apr 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Kazakhstan Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of National Economy Zhumangarin US Dept. of State 15 Apr 2026
  • Prime Minister receives call from US President Donald Trump, reviews bilateral cooperation and discusses West Asia and Strait of Hormuz India MEA 15 Apr 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 15 April 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 15 Apr 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Israel, Lebanon Talks Conclude In Washington, Signal Rare Common Ground RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- Israel and Lebanon concluded a rare round of direct talks in Washington on April 14 after roughly two hours, marking their first such engagement since 1983.

  • IDF Urges Evacuation As Strikes Continue in South Lebanon RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- Israel's military has issued a fresh evacuation order for residents of southern Lebanon as it pressed on with an offensive against Hezbollah -- a militant group and political party that controls much of southern Lebanon and is considered a terrorist organization by the United States, while the EU has blacklisted its armed wing.
  • Israel seeks Hezbollah disarmament, Lebanon calls for ceasefire in talks in U.S. People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- Israel and Lebanon laid out different expectations on a peace deal during talks here on Tuesday, with Israel insisting on Hezbollah's disarmament and Lebanon calling for a ceasefire and concrete measures to ease the severe humanitarian crisis resulting from the ongoing conflict, according to a statement from the U.S. State Department.

  • Hezbollah checks Israeli advance against southern Lebanon during fierce clashes Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Hezbollah says its fighters engaged in ongoing clashes with Israeli forces in the southern Lebanese town of Bint Jbeil and surrounding border areas, repelling attempts by Israeli troops to advance into the areas.
  • April 15: Hezbollah's operations against Zionist regime in response to truce breach Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Hours after Iran and the US announced a ceasefire ending 40 days of aggression against the Islamic Republic, the Israeli regime violated the truce last Wednesday morning by launching devastating strikes on civilian areas across Lebanon.

  • UN experts condemn Israel's unprecedented bombing in Lebanon after ceasefire announcement, demand immediate halt to hostilities OHCHR 15 Apr 2026 -- UN experts* today condemned Israel's illegal aggression and indiscriminate bombing campaign against Lebanon on 8 April, only hours after a ceasefire agreement was brokered by Pakistan between the United States and Iran.
  • UNHCR's Salih urges civilian protection and sustained support as escalation in Lebanon continues UNHCR 15 Apr 2026 -- Protection of civilians in Lebanon and sustained international support for the Government-led response to the country's huge displacement crisis are imperative, the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees, Barham Salih, said on Wednesday. One fifth of Lebanon's population is now displaced, with needs growing daily. Salih urged a quick end to the conflict before it spirals out of control.
  • UNRWA Situation Report #216 on the Humanitarian Crisis in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem) UNRWA 15 Apr 2026
  • UNRWA Situation Report #6 on the Lebanon Emergency Response 2026 (9 April 2026) UNRWA 15 Apr 2026

  • Trump Would Welcome 'End Of Hostilities' In Lebanon, US Official Says RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- The United States has not requested a Lebanon cease-fire, but President Donald Trump would welcome an end to the war in which Israel is targeting Iranian-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon, a senior US administration official said on April 15.

  • Potential Lebanon truce result of Hezbollah, Axis of Resistance's persistent struggle: Qalibaf Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- The speaker of Majlis (Iran's Parliament) says the emergence of a potential ceasefire in Lebanon will be down to the persistent struggle put up by Hezbollah and other members of the regional Axis of Resistance.

  • MoND's humanitarian actions in support of the civilian population in Lebanon Romania - Ministry of National Defence 15 Apr 2026 -- Two Romanian Air Force aircraft were scrambled on Wednesday, April 15, at around 10:15 a.m., from the 90th Air Base, in Otopeni, to airlift humanitarian aid for the civilian population of Lebanon, who is facing a humanitarian crisis.

  • 3 questions that could make or break Israel-Lebanon talks People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- Israeli and Lebanese representatives are holding direct talks in Washington on Tuesday, following more than a month of deadly fighting between the two neighbors and just one week after a U.S.-Israel-Iran ceasefire.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Sudan, Iran, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Central African Republic UNOCHA 15 Apr 2026

  • EU Statement - UN General Assembly: Prevention of armed conflict European External Action Service (EEAS) 15 Apr 2026 -- Statement on behalf of the European Union and its Member States by H.E. Ambassador Hedda Samson, Delegation of the European Union to the United Nations, at the United Nations General Assembly on Item 31 (a): Prevention of armed conflict
  • Funding Shortfalls Creating 'Blind Spots', Peacekeeping Chief Warns, Appeals to Security Council for 'Unequivocal' Political Support UN Security Council 15 Apr 2026 -- Amid a review of United Nations peacekeeping operations, the Security Council heard today that the challenging conditions in which these missions carry out the UN's core purpose of helping to "save succeeding generations from the scourge of war" are being made more complex due to severe financial constraints.
  • The United Kingdom condemns violence against UN peacekeepers who work tirelessly to protect international peace and security: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 15 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on UN Peacekeeping Operations.

  • Philippines Reaffirms Commitment to UNCLOS, 2016 South China Sea Arbitral Award During 9th Indian Ocean Conference in Mauritius Philippines DFA 15 Apr 2026 -- In his statement delivered before delegates of the 9th Indian Ocean Conference on 11 April 2026, Deputy Assistant Secretary and Spokesperson for Maritime Affairs Rogelio E. Villanueva, Jr. highlighted the 1982 United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) and the 2016 South China Sea Arbitral Award as the twin anchors of the country's foreign policy.
  • DFA Statement on the Use of Cyanide in Ayungin Shoal Philippines DFA 15 Apr 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs has taken note of the press conference yesterday of National Security Council (NSC) Assistant Director General and Spokesperson Cornelio H. Valencia regarding the illegal discharge and use of cyanide in Ayungin Shoal.
  • DFA condemns cyanide use at sea as UNCLOS violation Philippine News Agency 15 Apr 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Wednesday warned against the use of cyanide and other toxic agents at sea, saying this is "unacceptable" and a violation of international law, specifically the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).
  • PH calls on states for vigilance vs. efforts to undermine UNCLOS Philippine News Agency 15 Apr 2026 -- The Philippines is rallying maritime nations to maintain "vigilance against efforts to undermine international law," particularly the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).
  • Navy: No damage so far to BRP Sierra Madre from cyanide incident Philippine News Agency 15 Apr 2026 -- There are no signs of damage to the Philippine Navy ship BRP Sierra Madre (LS-57) stationed in Ayungin Shoal in the West Philippine Sea, despite reported Chinese attempts to spray cyanide within its vicinity.

  • 'Sudan is an atrocities laboratory', UN aid chief tells Berlin conference UN News 15 Apr 2026 -- Foreign ministers from across the world met in Berlin on Wednesday to show support for Sudan, where a fourth year of brutal warfare has begun as humanitarian needs deepen.
  • Secretary-General's video message to the third International Humanitarian Conference for Sudan UN Secretary-General 15 Apr 2026
  • UN Relief Chief: Sudan is an atrocities laboratory UNOCHA 15 Apr 2026 -- Remarks at International Humanitarian Conference for Sudan by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • EU and Member States pledge over €811 million in response to crisis in Sudan European Commission 15 Apr 2026
  • Co-Host Communiqué - International Sudan Conference Berlin Germany Federal Foreign Office 15 Apr 2026
  • Canada announces new support to address humanitarian crisis in Sudan Canada 15 Apr 2026 -- Canada is gravely concerned about the worsening humanitarian crisis in Sudan, driven by a civil war that began three years ago and has grown in scale and severity.
  • Backgrounder: New funding to address humanitarian crisis in Sudan and neighbouring countries Canada 15 Apr 2026
  • Five things you should know about Sudan's food crisis FAO 15 Apr 2026 -- In April 2023, violent conflict broke out between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces in the capital city, Khartoum, eventually ripping through the entire country. Three years on, Sudan is facing what is now verifiably the largest humanitarian crisis and the largest displacement crisis globally.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senators Booker, Coons, Merkley, Van Hollen Statement on the Third Anniversary of the Sudan Civil War US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 15 Apr 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Cory Booker (D-NJ), Ranking Member of the Africa and Global Health Policy Sub-Committee of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chris Coons (D-DE), Jeff Merkley (D-OR) and Chris Van Hollen (D-MD) issued a statement on the third anniversary of the start of the Sudanese Civil War urging all parties to take immediate action to end the suffering of the Sudanese people and for the Quad, led by the United States, Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Egypt, to push for a durable path forward with a peaceful transition of power.
  • Risch Statement on 3rd Anniversary of Sudan Conflict US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 15 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Senator Jim Risch (R-Idaho), chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, today released the statement below on the third anniversary of the war in Sudan.
  • UK boosts funding to local responders to help more than 1.8 million people in Sudan's humanitarian crisis UK FCDO 15 Apr 2026 -- UK to protect Sudan aid pledge and double funding to frontline responders as Foreign Secretary visits International Sudan Conference in Berlin.
  • Third anniversary of the outbreak of war in Sudan: Switzerland continues to provide humanitarian aid and seek a peaceful resolution to the conflict Federal Council - Swiss Government 15 Apr 2026 -- Switzerland is allocating around CHF 23 million in 2026 to support people in Sudan and the region with the humanitarian emergency they are facing. This was announced by Tim Enderlin, the head of the Peace and Human Rights Division at the FDFA and head of the Swiss delegation at the Sudan Conference in Berlin. Switzerland's support focuses on securing food supplies and livelihoods, the protection of the civilian population and peacebuilding. In December 2025, in view of the catastrophic humanitarian situation in Sudan and neighbouring countries - to which many people have fled from Sudan - Parliament approved an emergency aid credit of CHF 50 million.

  • Women's Leadership Key to Peace in Great Lakes Region, Security Council Hears as Rwanda, Democratic Republic of Congo Trade Barbs on Causes of Conflict UN Security Council 15 Apr 2026 -- Despite diplomatic progress aimed at ending the brutal conflict in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, senior officials today warned the Security Council that violence in the country's east continues unabated — with millions of women and girls disproportionately affected — and risks jeopardizing an otherwise positive trajectory in the wider Great Lakes region.
  • The United Kingdom remains concerned by the deteriorating humanitarian situation in the Great Lakes region: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 15 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Jennifer MacNaughtan, UK Minister Counsellor, at the Security Council meeting on the Great Lakes Region.

News Reports

  • Draft government proposal to update civilian intelligence legislation sent out for comments Finnish Government 14 Apr 2026 -- The Ministry of the Interior has sent out for comments a draft government proposal to update civilian intelligence legislation in response to the changed security environment.

  • IRGC intelligence forces capture 4 Mossad spies, dismantle terror teams in Iran Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) intelligence forces have captured four people in the northern province of Gilan on charges of spying for the Israeli spy agency Mossad, and dismantled terror teams in the southeastern part of the country.

  • Israel detains woman over alleged espionage for Iranian intelligence agencies Press TV 15 Apr 2026 -- Israel's so-called internal security service, known as Shin Bet or Shabak, has detained an Israeli woman over allegedly spying for Iranian intelligence agencies.

  • Russia and Belarus Intel Agencies Jointly Expose Malicious Foreign Plans - Russian Intel Head Sputnik 15 Apr 2026 -- "The situation on Russia's and Belarus's western borders can be described as tense," Sergei Naryshkin, director of Russia's Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR), said, speaking at a joint board meeting of the Russian and Belarusian intelligence services in Kaliningrad.

  • NASA Selects Voyager for Seventh Private Mission to Space Station NASA 15 Apr 2026 -- NASA and Voyager Technologies have signed an order for the seventh private astronaut mission to the International Space Station, targeted to launch no earlier than 2028 from Florida.

  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks during talks with President of the People's Republic of China Xi Jinping, Beijing, April 15, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks and answers to media questions following his visit to the People's Republic of China, Beijing, April 15, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 Apr 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's visit to the People's Republic of China Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 Apr 2026
  • Meeting on economic issues President of Russia 15 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a meeting on economic issues.
  • Briefing in the Russian Foreign Ministry for representatives of the embassies of BRICS member states and partner countries on the upcoming election of the UN Secretary-General Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 Apr 2026

  • Azerbaijan, Russia agree on resolving issues regarding AZAL plane crash Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 Apr 2026 -- "In accordance with agreements between the Presidents of the Republic of Azerbaijan and the Russian Federation during their meeting in Dushanbe on 9 October 2025, the Parties have reached an appropriate settlement of the consequences, including the issue of payment of compensation in connection with the crash of the Embraer 190 airplane belonging to Azerbaijan Airlines (AZAL) near the city of Aktau on 25 December 2024, resulting from the unintended operation of an air defense system in the airspace of the Russian Federation," the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Azerbaijan and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation said in a joint statement.
  • Joint Statement by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Azerbaijan Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 Apr 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on April 15, 2026 PRC MofA 15 Apr 2026
  • Soft power 'win' for Beijing as Chinese medical ship treats 5,400 for free in PNG Radio Free Asia 15 Apr 2026 -- Thousands of sick, disabled and otherwise unwell queued at Wharf T over the past week, hoping to board a Chinese hospital ship to receive free medical care.
  • China to strike back if U.S. imposes additional tariffs over so-called "military product exports": spokesperson People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- If the United States insists on imposing additional tariffs on China under the pretext of so-called "military product exports," China will take resolute countermeasures, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun told a daily press briefing on Tuesday.
  • Xi urges closer, stronger China-Russia strategic coordination to defend interests, uphold Global South unity Global Times 15 Apr 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday urged closer and stronger strategic coordination between China and Russia to firmly defend their legitimate interests and safeguard the unity of Global South countries.

  • Some individuals in US stir things up, repeatedly hype the so-called 'mainland threat or military pressure,' completely distorting facts and reflecting ulterior motives: spokesperson Global Times 15 Apr 2026 -- When asked to comment on the recent claim by Raymond Greene, Director of the Taipei Office of The American Institute in Taiwan (AIT), following Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's recent mainland visit, that the US has consistently hoped for stable cross-Straits interactions and expects the mainland to maintain communication channels with all political parties in the island of Taiwan, especially the so-called "democratically-elected government," while refraining from "threats or military pressure," Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said at a regular press conference on Wednesday that certain individuals in the US are stirring things up and repeatedly hyping the so-called "mainland threat or military pressure," which completely distorts the facts and reflects ulterior motives.
  • "Taiwan independence" will fail no matter how many weapons purchased: mainland spokesperson People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- A mainland spokesperson said on Wednesday that no matter how large a defense budget Taiwan's Lai Ching-te authorities allocate or how many weapons they purchase, it will not alter the eventual failure of their "Taiwan independence" attempts and may even accelerate it.
  • DPP authorities smear all mainland measures that benefit Taiwan compatriots; we urge them to listen more to public opinion on the island: mainland spokesperson Global Times 15 Apr 2026 -- In response to a question regarding the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities claimed that many of the mainland's 10 policies and measures to boost exchanges and cooperation across the Taiwan Straits amount to "instrumentalizing exchanges and weaponizing economic and trade ties as part of 'united front tactics,'" Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, at a regular press conference on Wednesday, advised the DPP authorities to listen more to public opinion in the island.
  • Some individuals in US stir things up, repeatedly hype the so-called 'mainland threat or military pressure,' completely distorting facts and reflecting ulterior motives: spokesperson Global Times 15 Apr 2026 -- When asked to comment on the recent claim by Raymond Greene, Director of the Taipei Office of The American Institute in Taiwan (AIT), following Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's recent mainland visit, that the US has consistently hoped for stable cross-Straits interactions and expects the mainland to maintain communication channels with all political parties in the island of Taiwan, especially the so-called "democratically-elected government," while refraining from "threats or military pressure," Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said at a regular press conference on Wednesday that certain individuals in the US are stirring things up and repeatedly hyping the so-called "mainland threat or military pressure," which completely distorts the facts and reflects ulterior motives.
  • 'Those who follow different paths cannot make plans together': mainland spokesperson responds as Lai declines to answer KMT chair Cheng's offer to meet before and after mainland visit Global Times 15 Apr 2026 -- Peace and war, dialogue and confrontation, cooperation and conflict are two fundamentally different paths, and "those who follow different paths cannot make plans together," by citing a well-known line from The Analects of Confucious, Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, responded to a media inquiry on that according to Taiwan regional media, chairwoman of the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party Cheng Li-wun has publicly expressed willingness to meet and communicate with Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te before and after her visit to the mainland, and has sincerely awaited an invitation from him, but has received no response.
  • Mainland pressing ahead with policy package to boost cross-Strait ties: spokesperson People's Daily 15 Apr 2026 -- The Chinese mainland is pressing ahead with the implementation of 10 newly released measures to boost exchanges and cooperation across the Taiwan Strait, a mainland spokesperson said on Wednesday.
  • 6 military personnel sentenced to prison in Chinese espionage case CNA 15 Apr 2026 -- The Taiwan High Court on Wednesday handed down prison sentences to six retired and active Taiwanese military personnel for leaking military secrets to China and gave them prison sentences of up to eight years and six months.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 15 Apr 2026

  • UN watchdog says North Korea is boosting nuclear weapons capacity Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 Apr 2026 -- North Korea has made "very serious" advances in its abilities to turn out nuclear weapons, with the probable addition of a new uranium enrichment facility, as it stepped up activity at a key complex, IAEA chief Rafael Grossi said on Wednesday, Reuters reported.
  • Two U.S. Nationals Sentenced for Facilitating Fraudulent Remote Information Technology Worker Scheme that Generated $5M in Revenue for the Democratic People's Republic of Korea US Dept. of Justice 15 Apr 2026 -- The Justice Department today announced the sentencings of two U.S. nationals, Kejia Wang, 42, and Zhenxing Wang, 39, for their roles in facilitating North Korean remote information technology (IT) workers posing as U.S. residents to obtain work at more than 100 U.S. companies.
  • Press Statement of Policy Section Chief of Institute for Japan Studies under DPRK Foreign Ministry KCNA 15 Apr 2026 -- The policy section chief of the Institute for Japan Studies under the Foreign Ministry of the DPRK issued a press statement titled "Japan's diplomatic blue book is a tricky document to beautify its extreme militarist moves by the 'threat from the neighboring countries'" on April 14.

  • Iran Sentences Four More To Death Over Mass Protests, Rights Groups Say RFE/RL 15 Apr 2026 -- Iran has sentenced four more protesters, including a woman, to death over mass demonstrations in January that posed one of the biggest threats to the country's clerical rulers in years, according to two human rights groups.

  • PM Netanyahu Speaks with Hungarian Prime Minister-elect Peter Magyar Israel - Prime Minister 15 Apr 2026

  • India - Germany Foreign office Consultations (April 14, 2026) India MEA 15 Apr 2026

  • Saudi Arabia to provide $3B to Pakistan amid debt repayment obligations to UAE Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 15 Apr 2026 -- Pakistan's finance minister confirmed late Tuesday that Saudi Arabia has agreed to provide $3 billion in financial assistance to Islamabad to avert stress on its weak foreign reserves amid debt repayment obligations to the United Arab Emirates, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • Japan-Bahrain Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 15 Apr 2026
  • Japan-Poland Summit Meeting and Working Lunch Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 15 Apr 2026

  • Press conference - Bandar Seri Begawan, Brunei Darussalam Prime Minister of Australia 15 Apr 2026

  • During His Briefing to the European Parliament's Committee on Foreign Affairs, HE GCCSG Commends the EU's Supportive Stance towards the GCC Regarding Iranian Aggression,Stressing that Gulf Security is Fundamental Pillar for the Stability of Global Economy Gulf Cooperation Council 15 Apr 2026
